summary: Iteration 46: P0 SCROLL BUG FORENSIC INVESTIGATION - All scroll tests PASSED on production URL (https://business-brain-redis.preview.emergentagent.com). Tested Desktop (1920x1080), Mobile (390x844), and Tablet (768x1024) viewports. window.scrollTo(), wheel events, and keyboard scroll all work correctly. CSS computed styles show overflow-y: scroll on html/body, no JavaScript scroll locks detected, no blocking inline styles. Page has sufficient scrollable content (Desktop: 5721px scrollHeight vs 1080px viewport, Mobile: 10086px vs 844px).
action_items: SCROLL WORKS IN TEST ENVIRONMENT - If user still experiences issues, check:
1. Browser cache - Hard refresh with Ctrl+Shift+R (Windows) or Cmd+Shift+R (Mac)
2. Service Workers - DevTools > Application > Service Workers > Unregister all
3. CDN/Edge cache - May take time to propagate new CSS
4. Browser extensions - Disable all extensions and test
5. Specific browser/device - Ask user which browser/OS/device they are using
6. Network proxy/firewall - Corporate networks may cache aggressively
critical_code_review_comments: PASS: scroll-fix-critical.css is imported LAST in index.js (line 9) - correct cascade order
PASS: scroll-fix-critical.css sets html/body overflow-y: auto !important with correct specificity
PASS: No inline styles blocking scroll detected
PASS: body.sidebar-open class correctly scoped - only locks scroll when sidebar open
PASS: Fixed background overlay has pointer-events: none - does not intercept scroll
PASS: touchAction: auto on body - mobile touch scroll not blocked
PASS: -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch enabled - iOS momentum scrolling works
PASS: No JavaScript event listeners calling preventDefault() on wheel/touchmove
INFO: Service worker kill code in index.js (lines 17-35) aggressively unregisters SW and clears caches
INFO: Same SW kill code duplicated in App.js useEffect (lines 145-162) - redundant but harmless

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All scroll tests passed on all viewports

context_for_next_testing_agent: Scroll is WORKING on production preview URL as of this test run. If user reports continued issues: (1) Ask for browser/OS/device details, (2) Ask user to open DevTools Console and check for errors, (3) Ask user to try Incognito/Private mode, (4) Check if issue is specific to certain scrollable sections. The scroll-fix-critical.css file is correctly imported and applied. All CSS computed styles are correct for scrolling.

rca_of_the_issue.original_report: User reported landing page scroll completely broken - could only use text highlight to navigate
investigation_findings: All programmatic scroll tests pass (scrollTo, wheel, keyboard)
CSS computed styles show overflow-y: scroll on html and body
No JavaScript scroll locks or inline style overrides detected
Page has significant scrollable content (4641px on desktop, 9242px on mobile)
Fixed overlay elements have pointer-events: none
touchAction: auto - mobile scroll not blocked
rca_of_the_issue.conclusion: CANNOT REPRODUCE - Scroll works correctly in Playwright browser automation tests on the production preview URL. The issue may be browser-specific, cache-related, or caused by external factors (extensions, network proxy).
recommended_user_actions: Clear browser cache and cookies for this domain
Try in a different browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
Check DevTools Console for JavaScript errors
